session_hint: the GitHub Actions CI build time investigation
question: What was causing the CI pipeline builds to take 45 minutes instead of 12?
turns: Our GitHub Actions CI pipeline ballooned from 12 minutes to 45 minutes over the past two weeks. First hypothesis: the Jest test suite isn't parallelized enough across runners.
Checked test step timing — tests run across 8 shards and finish in 6 minutes total. Shard utilization is even. Adding more shards won't help. Ruling out test parallelization as the bottleneck.

Second hypothesis: Docker layer cache is being busted on every run, forcing a full image rebuild. Checked the 'Use Cache' step output — layer cache hit rate is 94%, only the final app layer rebuilds. Docker build takes 3 minutes, same as before. Ruling out Docker layer cache.

Timed each step individually. The `npm ci` step takes 28 of the 45 minutes. The node_modules directory is never cached between pipeline runs — every build downloads all 1,847 packages from npm from scratch. Added actions/cache@v3 keyed on package-lock.json hash; cache hit brings npm ci down from 28 minutes to 8 seconds. That single change put total build time back to 11 minutes. Root cause: node_modules not cached between CI runs, npm ci running a full install on every build.
